message:
Improve binary verification timeout handling in update-cli

This commit replaces the spawn timeout option with a manual timeout
mechanism for more reliable cross-platform behavior when verifying
the downloaded CLI binary.

Problem:
The spawn() timeout option behavior varies across platforms and Node/Bun
versions. In some environments, it may not properly terminate hung
processes or may not work at all.

Solution:
Implement explicit timeout handling:
1. Create a 5-second timer using setTimeout
2. Track timeout state with a boolean flag
3. Kill the child process if timeout fires
4. Clear timeout on normal close or error events
5. Include timeout check in success condition

This ensures:
- Consistent 5-second timeout regardless of runtime
- Clean process termination on timeout
- Proper resource cleanup (timeout cleared)
- Accurate result: timedOut processes fail verification

The verification function now reliably handles slow or stuck binaries
without blocking the update process indefinitely.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
